Bacterial_Spot.symptoms: Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves that may have a yellow halo. Spots enlarge and become angular. Severely affected leaves turn yellow and drop.
Bacterial_Spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericides early in the season. Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Use disease-free seeds and resistant varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation to reduce leaf wetness.

Early_Blight.symptoms: Dark brown to black concentric rings (target-like spots) on older leaves. Lesions start on lower leaves and move upward. Yellowing around spots, premature leaf drop.
Early_Blight.treatment: Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil or mancozeb at first sign. Practice crop rotation (3-year cycle). Remove infected leaves immediately. Ensure proper plant spacing for air circulation.

Late_Blight.symptoms: Large, irregular, water-soaked, greenish-black lesions on leaves. White fuzzy mold growth on leaf undersides in humid conditions. Rapid plant collapse in severe cases.
Late_Blight.treatment: Apply systemic fungicides (metalaxyl or mefenoxam) preventively. Destroy all infected plants and tubers immediately. Avoid overhead watering. Plant certified disease-free seed potatoes.

Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Pale greenish-yellow spots on upper leaf surface. Olive-green to grayish-purple velvety mold on the underside. Leaves curl, wither, and drop in severe infections.
Leaf_Mold.treatment: Improve greenhouse ventilation and reduce humidity below 85%. Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil. Remove and destroy affected leaves. Stake plants to improve airflow.

Healthy.symptoms: No visible disease symptoms. Leaves are green, firm, and show normal growth patterns.
Healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Continue regular watering, fertilization, and monitoring. Maintain preventive measures like crop rotation and proper spacing.
